title: Remote Cluster Debugging (Engineering Tool)
content:
# Remote Cluster Debugging (Engineering Tool)

This guide describes how to use the `cluster-debug` tool to remotely inspect and debug customer k3s clusters via the Kelvin API — without SSH access.

## How It Works

The Kelvin API exposes a shell execution endpoint routed through `api-orchestration`:

```
POST /api/v1/orchestration/clusters/{cluster_name}/edge-apps/shell/exec
Body: { "command": "<shell command>" }
```

This sends the command to the `edge-updater` pod running on the customer cluster, which executes it as `sh -c <command>` and returns stdout/stderr. The `edge-updater` container has `kubectl` built in (same binary, symlinked), so any `kubectl` command works.

Since k3s runs as root, a privileged pod with `hostPID: true` gives full root shell access to the underlying node.

## Prerequisites

- Python 3 with `requests` installed
- A Kelvin API token (stored in OS keyring or passed via env var `KELVIN_TOKEN`)
- The target cluster name (as registered in the platform, e.g. `mycluster`)

## Tool Location

```
support-and-monitoring/tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py
```

## Basic Usage

### Run a kubectl command
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster kubectl get pods -A
```

### Get pod logs
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster logs -n app my-pod-name
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster kubectl logs -n app my-pod-name --tail=200
```

### Run arbitrary shell command
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster exec 'df -h'
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster exec 'cat /etc/os-release'
```

### Deploy the privileged debug pod
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster deploy-debug
```
This deploys `kelvin-debug` pod in the `kelvin` namespace with:
- `privileged: true`
- `hostPID: true`
- `hostNetwork: true`
- Host root filesystem mounted at `/host`

### Run a command on the host (via nsenter)
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster host 'journalctl -u k3s --no-pager -n 50'
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster host 'crictl ps'
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster host 'free -h && df -h'
```

### Interactive REPL
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster shell
```
Enter kubectl or shell commands one at a time. Type `exit` to quit.

### Clean up the debug pod
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster cleanup
```

## Common Debug Patterns

### Check workload status
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl get pods -A
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl describe pod -n app <pod-name>
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl get events -n app --sort-by=.lastTimestamp
```

### Get logs from a stuck workload
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl logs -n app <pod> --previous
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl logs -n app <pod> --tail=500
```

### Check node and cluster health
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl get nodes -o wide
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl top nodes
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl top pods -A
```

### Inspect edge services
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl get pods -n kelvin
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl logs -n kelvin -l app=edge-k8s --tail=100
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl logs -n kelvin -l app=edge-updater --tail=100
```

### Check staged workloads (common cause of pending-deploy issues)
```bash
# List all staged workloads in edge-k8s SQLite DB
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> kubectl exec -n kelvin deploy/edge-k8s -- sqlite3 /data/edge-k8s.db 'SELECT * FROM staged_workloads;'
```

### Host-level inspection (requires debug pod)
```bash
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> deploy-debug
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> host 'systemctl status k3s'
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> host 'journalctl -u k3s -n 100 --no-pager'
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> host 'crictl ps -a'
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py <cluster> cleanup
```

## Authentication

The tool reads the Kelvin API token from:
1. OS keyring (service: `kelvin-api`, key: `token`) — preferred
2. Environment variable: `KELVIN_TOKEN`
3. Interactive prompt as fallback

To store your token in the keyring:
```python
import keyring
keyring.set_password('kelvin-api', 'token', '<your-token>')
```

## Limitations

- **Not interactive**: The shell/exec endpoint is request/response. Long-running or interactive commands (top, watch, vim) will time out or not work. Use the REPL for iterative investigation.
- **Timeout**: Commands time out after ~30 seconds by default. For long operations (log dumps), pipe through `head` or use `--tail`.
- **No streaming**: Large log outputs are returned in full. Use `--tail` to limit.
- **Cluster must be online**: The cluster must have an active NATS connection to the cloud. Offline clusters cannot receive commands.
summary: How to use the cluster-debug tool to remotely inspect and debug customer k3s clusters via the Kelvin API shell/exec endpoint, including deploying a privileged debug pod for host-level access.

code_examples.code: python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster kubectl get pods -A
python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster deploy-debug && python3 tools/cluster-debug/cluster_debug.py mycluster host 'journalctl -u k3s -n 50 --no-pager'
